title: relations.json has no scoped remove/replace edge op — edge hygiene is append-only through the tool surface
description: append-relation is a full dual-surface op (library appendRelation + Pi tool + orchestrator script + CLI). There is no counterpart to remove or replace a single edge. Whole-file writeRelations exists (library only, consumed by rename-canonical-id) but there is no scoped remove-relation primitive, no Pi tool, and no CLI op. So a mis-filed edge (wrong direction / type / endpoint — e.g. the TASK-004/TASK-005 dependency just corrected) cannot be fixed through the operator/agent tool surface; only ad-hoc loadRelations+filter+writeRelations library code can, which harness-confined in-pi agents (registered Pi tools only) cannot run.

evidence.reference: append-relation is dual-surface (library appendRelation + Pi tool + CLI + script); no remove/replace counterpart exists anywhere
writeRelations is a whole-file replace (library only, used by rename-canonical-id); grep for removeRelation/deleteRelation/pruneRelation returns none — no scoped single-edge removal
the backwards TASK-005->TASK-004 edge was removable only via orchestrator-side loadRelations+filter+writeRelations (5621c2a), unavailable to cli-only or in-pi-tool-only consumers

impact: Edges are append-only through tools; mis-directions, typos, and superseded edges accumulate uncorrectably for cli-only and harness-confined consumers, who would need privileged library access to fix them — breaking the dual-surface symmetry append-relation otherwise has.
proposed_resolution: A scoped remove-relation (and replace-relation) as a dual-surface unit mirroring append-relation: library removeRelation(cwd, {parent, child, relation_type}) (idempotent whole-file rewrite minus the matched edge) + Pi tool + CLI op + orchestrator script; run context-validate after for referential integrity. replace-relation = remove+append in one call for direction/type corrections.
